Last updated April 26, 2026

BodyPilot Privacy Policy

BodyPilot is a health and fitness coaching workspace for food logging, training, check-ins, readiness, schedule planning, coaching notes, and physique-prep decision support. We treat this information as sensitive user data.

Open app Privacy choices Terms Support

Data BodyPilot handles

Health and fitness data

Nutrition, macros, bodyweight, progress notes, recovery, training, steps, check-ins, photos, and related coaching context.

Account and contact data

Email address, display name, role, local sign-in state, verification state, and support messages if you contact us.

Coaching data

Coach-client invites, plan versions, messages, delivery receipts, change summaries, review trails, and adherence context.

Device and app data

Local settings, notification preferences, backup files you export, restore previews, and basic app recovery status.

How data is stored and used

In the current version, BodyPilot stores app data locally on the user's device unless the user exports a backup file or contacts support. Data is used to provide app functionality: logging, planning, recommendations, check-in review, coach-client continuity, reminders, backup/restore, and account deletion.

BodyPilot does not sell user data, does not use behavioral advertising, and does not use third-party tracking SDKs in this build. If production cloud sync, analytics, billing, or third-party services are added, the App Store privacy details and this policy must be updated before release.

User choices

  • Export a copy of local BodyPilot data from Settings, Data.
  • Delete an account from Settings, Account or Settings, Privacy.
  • Reset local workspace data from Settings, Data.
  • Turn reminder categories and notification channels on or off from Settings, Notifications.
  • Contact support for privacy requests at support@bodypilot.app.

Health and safety

BodyPilot is for logging, planning, coaching context, and performance review. It is not medical advice, diagnosis, treatment, emergency guidance, or an automated dosing system. Users should consult qualified healthcare professionals before changing training, nutrition, supplement, or compound protocols.
